evals.name: full-sweep
evals.expected_output: Full migration applying all better-result-adopt rules

assertions.description: DB insert try/catch in createUser uses Result.tryPromise, not try/catch
Async operations that can fail wrapped with Result.tryPromise
Null checks (user.rows[0], cart) return Result.err, not throw
Domain errors use TaggedError classes (ValidationError, NotFoundError, etc.) not plain Error
Original error preserved as cause in TaggedError (especially the DB insert catch)
Return types changed to Result<T, E> or Promise<Result<T, E>>
processCheckout (3+ sequential Results) uses Result.gen with yield*, not manual if(isErr)
Each Result-returning call in processCheckout uses yield* (with Result.await for async)
add() and validateEmail() are NOT wrapped in Result — they are pure functions that never fail
handleCreateUser uses .match() or Result pattern, not try/catch around Result-returning functions
No instance of `return Result.err(result.error)` — returns the result directly if propagating
Does not catch all exceptions broadly — PaymentError catch is explicit, unexpected errors are not swallowed
Migration starts at I/O boundaries (db.query, chargePayment), not at pure utilities
